vulnerability.title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Minimum Length
vulnerability.description: The pam_pwquality module's minlen parameter controls requirements for minimum characters required in a password. Add minlen= after pam_pwquality to set minimum password length requirements.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q libpwquality; }; then var_password_pam_minlen='' if grep -sq minlen /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf ; then sed -i "/minlen/d" /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf fi # Strip any search characters in the key arg so that the key can be replaced without # adding any search characters to the config file. stripped_key=$(sed 's/[\^=\$,;+]*//g' <<< "^minlen") # shellcheck disable=SC2059 printf -v formatted_output "%s = %s" "$stripped_key" "$var_password_pam_minlen" # If the key exists, change it. Otherwise, add it to the config_file. # We search for the key string followed by a word boundary (matched by \>), # so if we search for 'setting', 'setting2' won't match. if LC_ALL=C grep -q -m

triage_decision.reason: The finding is related to password policy, which is considered low-risk and unlikely to lock out access.

review_feedback: The remediation does not fully enforce the minlen password requirement; it modifies the PAM line but fails to set minlen in the pwquality configuration, leaving the vulnerability unresolved. Suggestions: Directly configure minlen=8 in /etc/security/pwquality.conf or a file under pwquality.conf.d using a controlled method.; Employ a configuration management tool (e.g., Ansible, Puppet) to ensure idempotent application.; Validate the resulting configuration with a syntax check and a test password change.; Avoid parsing grep output; use line-based insertion with sed or awk that guarantees exact key replacement.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation does not fully enforce the minlen password requirement; it modifies the PAM line but fails to set minlen in the pwquality configuration, leaving the vulnerability unresolved.
concerns: Minlen parameter is not correctly added to the pwquality configuration file; only a PAM module line is altered.
Use of sed to inject 'minlen=8' may introduce syntax errors or affect other PAM services.
The script removes existing minlen entries without ensuring a replacement value is added, risking incomplete configuration.
No validation or testing is performed after modifications to confirm the setting is applied correctly.
suggested_improvements: Directly configure minlen=8 in /etc/security/pwquality.conf or a file under pwquality.conf.d using a controlled method.
Employ a configuration management tool (e.g., Ansible, Puppet) to ensure idempotent application.
Validate the resulting configuration with a syntax check and a test password change.
Avoid parsing grep output; use line-based insertion with sed or awk that guarantees exact key replacement.
